channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The death toll in the North Macedonia nightclub fire rises to 60 after a burn victim dies
item.description: SKOPJE, North Macedonia (AP) — A critically injured burn victim from a nightclub fire last month in North Macedonia has died while receiving treatment in Lithuania, raising the death toll to 60, the country’s health minister said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In warning to Turkey, Israel hits strategic targets in Syria amid rising tensions
item.description: Israel targets Syrian airbases to warn Turkey against interference, as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan faces protests at home and ramps up efforts to expand influence in Syria and region as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Danish leader tells the US 'you cannot annex another country' as she visits Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Denmark's prime minister has told the U.S. during a visit to Greenland that “you cannot annex another country,” even with the argument that international security is at stake. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Orbán says Hungary is quitting the ICC to end its 'half-hearted' membership
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary was never fully committed to the International Criminal Court, Prime Minister Viktor Orbán said on Friday, a day after his government announced a decision to quit the global tribunal for war crimes and genocide.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 17 in Gaza as ground troops enter Palestinian territory's north
item.description: Israeli strikes have killed more than a dozen people in the Gaza Strip as Israel sends more ground troops into the Palestinian territory to ramp up its offensive against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China imposing 34 percent tariffs on all US goods
item.description: China, in the wake of President Trump's latest tariffs on foreign trading partners, announced Friday it will hit the U.S. with a 34 percent reciprocal tax on imports starting next week.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Sri Lanka's target to be free of land mines by 2028 is under threat as US reviews aid
item.description: MANKULAM, Sri Lanka (AP) — Thavarathnam Pushparani fought on the front lines for the now-defeated Tamil Tiger rebels against the Sri Lankan forces in its decadeslong separatist war and later took to clearing the land mines on the same battle lines.
item.dc:creator: ERANGA JAYAWARDENA and KRISHAN FRANCIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Europe and Canada say they'll spend more on defense, but are cool on US demands
item.description: European NATO allies and Canada say they will ramp up defense spending but are cool on U.S. demands for the size of their military budgets.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A week after catastrophic earthquake, focus turns to a growing humanitarian crisis in Myanmar
item.description: Search teams have pulled more bodies from the ruins of buildings a week after a massive earthquake rocked Myanmar killing more than 3,300 people.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Boasberg floats contempt hearings on deportation flights, suspects 'bad faith' actions from Trump admin
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg hinted at possible contempt proceedings as he grilled a government lawyer Thursday about whether the Trump administration violated his court order by failing to turn around planes carrying migrants to a Salvadoran prison.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Myanmar earthquake death toll rises to 3,145 as more bodies found
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from the earthquake that hit Myanmar nearly a week ago rose Thursday to 3,145 as search and rescue teams found more bodies, the military-led government said, and humanitarian aid groups scrambled to provide survivors medical care and shelter.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump levies tariffs on uninhabited islands
item.description: President Trump, as part of his expansive package of new tariffs, levied taxes on a number of uninhabited or sparsely populated islands that have little-to-no exports. The announcement of new tariffs on Wednesday will hit nearly all foreign countries with a baseline tax at 10 percent.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Burma earthquake death toll rises to 3,085, humanitarian crisis deepens
item.description: More than 3,000 people have died from the 7.7 magnitude earthquake that struck Burma last week, which has sent the country into a deeper humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EU Parliament has transparency problems. Marine Le Pen's case is a window into what's wrong
item.description: The conviction of one of the most powerful figures of the European far right for embezzling EU Parliament funds has sent shockwaves around the continent and beyond. But Marine Le Pen's case is just one example of transparency problems that have plagued the legislature.
item.dc:creator: ELLA JOYNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu is rocked by a new scandal linking his close advisers to Qatar
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office is once again ensnared in scandal after police arrested two of his close associates this week on suspicion of accepting money from Qatar to promote a positive image of the Gulf Arab state in Israel.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's PM Carney vows to ‘fight’ Trump’s tariffs, other world leaders weigh impact
item.description: President Trump's newest tariffs sparked pledges by Canada's prime minster to 'fight' back while some said they were committed to working with partners and others look to take on Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: “Tragically, three soldiers were found deceased in Lithuania yesterday, and it pains me to confirm that today the fourth soldier was also found deceased,” White House said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Iran mulls preemptive strike on US base after Trump bomb threats
item.description: Iran is considering its options in deterring President Trump amid his repeated threats of direct military engagement by possibly launching its own strike on a U.S. base in the Indian Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Survivors still being found from Burma earthquake, but hopes begin to fade as deaths exceed 2,700
item.description: Burma was hit by a 7.7 magnitude earthquake on Friday, killing more than 2,700 people, compounding a humanitarian crisis caused by a civil war. Thailand also sustained damage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Earthquake compounds Myanmar's humanitarian crisis as the death toll passes 2,000
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll in last week's massive earthquake in Myanmar has passed 2,000, state media said Monday, as accounts of some people's last moments emerged: Two hundred Buddhist monks crushed by a collapsing monastery. Fifty children killed when a preschool classroom crumbled.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to expect in South Korea now that Yoon has been removed from office
item.description: South Korea’s Constitutional Court has formally removed President Yoon Suk Yeol from office by upholding his impeachment over his imposition of martial law in December.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bangkok reviews construction safety after a high-rise was crumpled by an earthquake 800 miles away
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai authorities said Monday they are investigating why a single office tower block under construction in Bangkok collapsed during Friday's earthquake that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital. Bangkok Gov.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Finnish president: Trump should give Putin 3 weeks to agree Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: Finnish President Alexander Stubb told Fox News that President Trump should impose an April 20 deadline for his Russian counterpart, Vladimir Putin, to agree a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600, leaves others buried
item.description: The 7.7 magnitude quake hit midday Friday with an epicenter near Mandalay, bringing down scores of buildings and damaging other infrastructure like the city’s airport.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs, says he is 'pissed off' at Putin
item.description: President Trump also threatened major new sanctions against Russia if he believes it is Vladimir Putin's fault for failing to reach a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation
